title: INF-1 LoRA hot-swap integration test (parity across LlamaCpp + Candle)
goal: Cross-adapter parity test: same LoRA descriptor mounted on a LlamaCpp model and a Candle model produces equivalent qualitative behavior (output differs from base on both; license tag preserved; FR events emit on both). Catches per-adapter drift.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/tests/inf1_lora_parity_tests.rs exercising mount+generate on both adapter paths against a parallel fixture model pair.
implementation_notes: Per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.a parity gate: behavior must be uniform across both adapters for INF-1 PRODUCTION declaration to hold. Test scenario: register two models — one LlamaCpp (GGUF), one Candle (safetensors) — both fine-tunes of the same base; mount a LoRA designed for that base on each; generate; assert both differ from base; assert license tag carried through; assert FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-LORA-MOUNT fires on both. Skip gracefully when env vars unset. Cross-adapter equivalence is qualitative (not bit-exact; different sampler RNG paths) — assert e.g., perplexity-diff > threshold on a fixed prompt. This is the regression gate for INF-1 across adapter boundary.
risk_if_missed: Adapter drift in INF-1 unnoticed; LoRA behavior unstable across adapters.
validator_focus: Skip path documented; qualitative parity threshold reasonable; license tag carried.

minimum_controls: License tag preservation verified across adapter boundary.
